articles.title: Amazon, Meta join fight to end Google Pay, PhonePe dominance in India
articles.description: Amazon and Meta are among the big companies set to lobby India's payments body over the dominance of Walmart-owned PhonePe and Google Pay in the country's fast-growing instant payments network.
articles.author: Jagmeet Singh
articles.content: Executives representing platforms including Amazon Pay, WhatsApp, CRED, MobiKwik, and Flipkart's Super.money are scheduled to meet the National Payments Corporation of India. The body operates the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), India's instant payments system that processes billions of transactions each month. PhonePe and Google Pay combined accounted for roughly 80% of the 22.6 billion transactions on the UPI network in March. PhonePe said this week it has crossed 700 million registered users and 50 million merchants across India, highlighting the reach that smaller rivals say is difficult to replicate. An agenda reviewed by TechCrunch shows participants are expected to raise concerns about user acquisition practices, product design, and monetization within the UPI ecosystem. Among the proposals are restrictions on how dominant apps onboard users and use contact data, calls for fair access to features such as autopay and payment mandates, and requests for incentives and regulatory support to help emerging players compete.

articles.title: Amazon's cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
articles.description: Amazon was one of several tech giants that on Wednesday beat Wall Street's first-quarter earnings expectations, offering more financial evidence that the AI boom continues to reward companies that supply the picks and shovels.
articles.author: Lucas Ropek
articles.content: Amazon Web Services, buoyed by its role in fueling the AI boom, saw its net sales increase 28% year-over-year, climbing to $37.6 billion. It was the fastest growth rate for AWS in 15 quarters. CEO Andy Jassy said: "It's very unusual for business to grow this fast on a base this large. We've never seen a technology grow as rapidly as AI." Even as money flows into its cloud business, Amazon is also sinking increasingly large gobs of capital into building out the infrastructure that supports that cloud. "The faster AWS grows, the more short-term capex we'll spend," Jassy said. "AWS has to lay out cash for land, power, buildings, chips, servers, and networking gear, in advance of when we can monetize it." Amazon's overall sales rose 17% to $181.5 billion on a year-over-year basis.

articles.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
articles.description: The maker of the Claude AI assistant has received multiple preemptive offers to raise fresh capital of around $50 billion at a valuation in the $850 billion to $900 billion range.
articles.author: Marina Temkin
articles.content: Investor interest in Anthropic has reached a feverish pitch. Sources say Anthropic is finding it difficult to resist the pressure to secure more funding in what could be its final round of private fundraising before a potential IPO. The round is expected to total $40 billion to $50 billion. Anthropic announced this month that its annual revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion, a dramatic increase from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. A large portion of that revenue is driven by Anthropic's AI coding capabilities, specifically through its Claude Code and Cowork platforms. Anthropic raised its last round at a $380 billion valuation in February. If the company proceeds with another fundraise at the terms described, it will more than double its valuation and match or surpass that of its chief rival OpenAI.

articles.title: How Medium uses AI at work: Humans first, especially in the age of AI
articles.description: Grounding our AI use in fairness, accountability, care, transparency, and stewardship. Humans first, especially in the age of AI.
articles.author: Brittany Jezouit
articles.content: At Medium, we've spent considerable time thinking about how to use AI responsibly in our workplace. Our framework centers on five core principles: fairness, accountability, care, transparency, and stewardship. We believe AI should augment human creativity and judgment, not replace it. Our editorial team uses AI-assisted tools for research and grammar checking, but all editorial decisions — what stories to surface, how to present them, whose voices to amplify — remain firmly human. We've also established clear disclosure policies: any content with significant AI involvement is clearly labeled. This approach has helped us maintain reader trust while allowing our team to focus on higher-value creative work.

articles.title: Transform Voices with AI: A Complete Guide to Seed-VC
articles.description: Learn how to perform high-quality voice conversion using zero-shot learning. A complete guide to Seed-VC.
articles.author: Ben
articles.content: Seed-VC is a state-of-the-art voice conversion model that allows you to transform any voice into another using just a few seconds of reference audio. Unlike traditional voice cloning that requires hours of training data, Seed-VC leverages zero-shot learning — meaning it can adapt to a new target voice it has never seen before. The model works by disentangling the linguistic content of speech from the speaker's identity, then reconstructing the audio with the new speaker characteristics. This guide walks you through setting up the environment, preparing your audio samples, running inference, and fine-tuning the output quality for professional results.

articles.title: Up and Running with Autonomous Mobile Robotics (AMR) in ROS 2 and C++
articles.description: For any physically-intelligent mobile robot that operates autonomously in the real world, successful navigation is a critical capability.
articles.author: Yasin Yousif
articles.content: Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) rely on a navigation stack that combines sensor fusion, localization, path planning, and obstacle avoidance. In ROS 2, the Nav2 framework provides all these capabilities out of the box, but integrating them correctly requires a solid understanding of the underlying architecture. This guide covers setting up a differential drive robot with a 2D lidar sensor, configuring the SLAM Toolbox for mapping, and tuning the DWB (Dynamic Window Approach B) local planner for smooth, collision-free navigation. All code is written in C++ using the rclcpp client library, targeting ROS 2 Humble.

articles.title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
articles.description: During a contentious court hearing, Elon Musk testified that his AI company xAI used outputs from OpenAI's models to train its Grok chatbot, potentially exposing xAI to significant legal liability.
articles.author: Tim Fernholz
articles.content: In a surprising revelation during ongoing litigation between OpenAI and its early backers, Elon Musk admitted under oath that xAI, his artificial intelligence startup, used data generated by OpenAI's GPT models as training signal for Grok. OpenAI's terms of service explicitly prohibit using its outputs to train competing models. The testimony adds a new dimension to an already complicated legal battle that centers on whether OpenAI has drifted from its original nonprofit mission. Legal analysts say the admission could expose xAI to a breach-of-contract claim, though the exact terms of any access Musk may have had during his time on OpenAI's board remain disputed.

articles.title: Ouster's new color lidar is coming to replace cameras
articles.description: Lidar startup Ouster unveiled a new sensor capable of capturing color point clouds, a breakthrough that could finally make lidar a viable replacement for cameras in autonomous vehicles and robotics.
articles.author: Sean O'Kane
articles.content: Ouster's new REV7 sensor captures rich color data alongside depth information, producing vibrant 3D point clouds that were previously only achievable by fusing separate camera and lidar data streams. The sensor achieves this by embedding photodetectors tuned to different wavelengths directly into the chip. For autonomous vehicle developers, this simplifies the sensor stack significantly — one sensor can now do the job of two, reducing cost, latency, and the complexity of sensor fusion algorithms. Ouster says the REV7 will be available for developer sampling in Q3 2026, with mass production planned for early 2027.

articles.title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
articles.description: Uber is pitching a new data-collection service that would use its fleet of human drivers to generate the real-world driving data that autonomous vehicle companies desperately need.
articles.author: Connie Loizos
articles.content: Uber is quietly pitching a new business to autonomous vehicle companies: a fleet-wide sensor network that would outfit its human drivers' cars with dashcams and other sensors to capture high-quality, geo-tagged driving data at scale. AV companies spend enormous sums collecting edge-case driving data — the rare but critical scenarios like unusual weather, unexpected pedestrian behavior, or road damage — that are needed to train and validate their systems. Uber's pitch is that its millions of drivers, operating in hundreds of cities around the world, are already encountering these scenarios every day. The company believes it can monetize this data as a subscription service to AV firms.

articles.title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
articles.description: Skio, a Shopify subscription platform founded by a Y Combinator alum, has been acquired for $105 million in an all-cash deal — a remarkable outcome for a company that raised only $8 million in venture funding.
articles.author: Julie Bort
articles.content: Skio founder Kennan Davison announced the acquisition on social media, calling it a "dream outcome" for his small, capital-efficient team. The company built a subscription management platform on top of Shopify that competed directly with giants like Recharge by focusing on a smoother customer experience and lower churn. Davison said the key to their success was staying lean — the company never had more than 15 employees — and obsessing over product quality. The $105M all-cash deal represents a 13x return on invested capital for their investors, an exceptional multiple in the current market. The acquirer was not disclosed at the time of announcement.
